title: Constants, Literals, Domain Types, and Configuration · Mastery Check
summary: Replace ambiguous magic values without creating meaningless constant noise. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a domain constant and configuration policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish harmless local literals from repeated domain rules, units, limits, and protocol values.
Name stable domain values by meaning and keep them near their owning policy.
Use enums, unions, literal types, branded values, or value objects for closed domains.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Literal Risk · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: gates constants literals. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: gates constants literals. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Literal Risk without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish harmless local literals from repeated domain rules, units, limits, and protocol values.

content.text: Distinguish harmless local literals from repeated domain rules, units, limits, and protocol values. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates constants literals. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Literal Risk, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a domain constant and configuration policy.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Every numeric or string literal must become a constant. This fails the Literal Risk contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Clang AddressSanitizer and UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer (Clang 23 development documentation) — Controls native-code runtime sanitizer purpose, build profiles, output, limitations, and security cautions.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish harmless local literals from repeated domain rules, units, limits, and protocol values.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Constant Tests applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test boundary values and downstream behavior instead of only constant equality.
